title: Write about the following topic:
prompt: Some people say that advertising is extremely successful at persuading us to buy things. Other people think that advertising is so common that we no longer pay attention to it. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.

paragraphs.text: Write about the following topic:
Some people say that advertising is extremely successful at persuading us to buy things. Other people think that advertising is so common that we no longer pay attention to it.
paragraphs.italic_text: Some people say that advertising is extremely successful at persuading us to buy things. Other people think that advertising is so common that we no longer pay attention to it.

paragraphs.text: Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.
paragraphs.italic_text: Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.

paragraphs.text: Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
Write at least 250 words.
Topic: advertising
View 1: successful at persuading customers
View 2: no longer pay attention
High-quality advertising still persuasive, but most common advertising no long draw our attention
1: Advertising is so common
Constant exposure (attention divided)
People become numb
2: why still can be successful
High-quality advertising
Celebrity / algorism -based recommendations (personalization)
A number of people deem advertising extremely successful at persuading us to make purchases, while others believe that we no longer pay attention to advertisements because they have become so common.
This essay argues that while most advertisements are often ignored due to constant exposure, well-designed advertising is still very persuasive.
This essay argues that although many advertisements are ignored due to constant exposure, well-designed advertisements remain highly persuasive.
paragraphs.italic_text: This essay argues that although many advertisements are ignored due to constant exposure, well-designed advertisements remain highly persuasive.

paragraphs.text: Admittedly, the Internet has revolutionized the advertising industry, allowing a vast number of advertisements to fill almost every spare moment of our life.
Nevertheless, constant exposure under various kinds of ads may backfire.
People are likely to become numb and even develop negative attitudes towards the advertising.
For example, in my home country, China, almost every applications and websites have pop-out advertisements at the moment of opening.
For example, in my home country, China, almost all applications and websites display pop-up advertisements when they are opened.
paragraphs.italic_text: For example, in my home country, China, almost all applications and websites display pop-up advertisements when they are opened.

paragraphs.text: A huge proportion of users may skip the ads immediately, some of them may even use the ads blocker to screen out all the advertising.
A large proportion of users skip these advertisements immediately, and some even use ad blockers to filter them out entirely.
paragraphs.italic_text: A large proportion of users skip these advertisements immediately, and some even use ad blockers to filter them out entirely.

paragraphs.text: However, those well-planed advertisements are still able to persuade customers to buy things, especially with the help of the Internet.
However, well-planned advertisements are still capable of persuading consumers to make purchases, particularly with the help of the Internet.
paragraphs.italic_text: However, well-planned advertisements are still capable of persuading consumers to make purchases, particularly with the help of the Internet.

paragraphs.text: Since most people usually spent a great deal of time browsing online, their behavior pattern and habits are recorded by advertising companies which will be used to train models and improve algorisms.
Since most people spend a great deal of time browsing online, their behaviour patterns are recorded by advertising companies and used to train models and improve algorithms.
paragraphs.italic_text: Since most people spend a great deal of time browsing online, their behaviour patterns are recorded by advertising companies and used to train models and improve algorithms.

paragraphs.text: Personalized algorism-based recommendations can effectively lower the consumers’ resistance and entice them to make the purchase.
Personalised algorithm-based recommendations can effectively reduce consumer resistance and encourage purchases.
paragraphs.italic_text: Personalised algorithm-based recommendations can effectively reduce consumer resistance and encourage purchases.

paragraphs.text: In addition, some advertising companies may employ the endorsements of celebrities and social influencers to enhance their credibility in order to make their advertisements more appealing.
In addition, companies often use celebrity and social media endorsements to enhance credibility and make their advertisements more appealing.
paragraphs.italic_text: In addition, companies often use celebrity and social media endorsements to enhance credibility and make their advertisements more appealing.

paragraphs.text: In conclusion, while many individuals are drowning in advertisements of all kinds, it is improper to claim that people can resist the impulse of shopping in front of all advertising.
In conclusion, although people are exposed to a vast number of advertisements, it is incorrect to assume that they can always resist the urge to buy.
paragraphs.italic_text: In conclusion, although people are exposed to a vast number of advertisements, it is incorrect to assume that they can always resist the urge to buy.

paragraphs.text: Well-designed advertisements are still very persuasive with the help of modern technologies like recommending models and algorisms. (284 words)
Well-designed advertisements remain highly persuasive, particularly with the support of modern technologies such as recommendation systems and algorithms.
paragraphs.italic_text: Well-designed advertisements remain highly persuasive, particularly with the support of modern technologies such as recommendation systems and algorithms.

paragraphs.text: Focus on:
Eliminating small grammar errors
Using more natural academic phrasing
Improving sentence fluency

Some people believe that advertising is highly effective in persuading consumers to purchase products, while others argue that it has become so widespread that people tend to ignore it. This essay will discuss both views and argue that although excessive advertising reduces its overall impact, well-targeted and carefully designed advertisements remain highly influential.

On the one hand, the sheer volume of advertising in modern life has led to a degree of consumer fatigue. With the rise of the Internet, individuals are constantly exposed to advertisements on websites, social media platforms, and mobile applications. As a result, many people have become desensitised to such content and may even develop negative attitudes towards it. For instance, in countries like China, it is common for applications and websites to display multiple pop-up advertisements upon opening. Consequently, users often skip these advertisements immediately or install ad-blocking software to avoid them altogether. This suggests that excessive exposure can significantly reduce the effectiveness of advertising.

On the other hand, not all advertising is ignored, particularly when it is strategically tailored to individual consumers. Advances in data collection and algorithmic technology enable companies to analyse users’ online behaviour and deliver highly personalised advertisements. Such targeted content is more likely to capture attention and influence purchasing decisions. Furthermore, many companies enhance the appeal of their advertisements by using endorsements from celebrities and social media influencers, which can increase credibility and trust. As a result, consumers may be more inclined to engage with and respond to these advertisements.

In conclusion, while the overabundance of advertising has led many people to disregard it, this does not mean that advertising has lost its persuasive power entirely. In my view, well-designed and targeted advertising continues to play a significant role in shaping consumer behaviour, especially in the digital age.(297)

model_essay_paragraphs: Some people believe that advertising is highly effective in persuading consumers to purchase products, while others argue that it has become so widespread that people tend to ignore it. This essay will discuss both views and argue that although excessive advertising reduces its overall impact, well-targeted and carefully designed advertisements remain highly influential.

On the one hand, the sheer volume of advertising in modern life has led to a degree of consumer fatigue. With the rise of the Internet, individuals are constantly exposed to advertisements on websites, social media platforms, and mobile applications. As a result, many people have become desensitised to such content and may even develop negative attitudes towards it. For instance, in countries like China, it is common for applications and websites to display multiple pop-up advertisements upon opening. Consequently, users often skip these advertisements immediately or install ad-blocking software to avoid them altogether. This suggests that excessive exposure can significantly reduce the effectiveness of advertising.

On the other hand, not all advertising is ignored, particularly when it is strategically tailored to individual consumers. Advances in data collection and algorithmic technology enable companies to analyse users’ online behaviour and deliver highly personalised advertisements. Such targeted content is more likely to capture attention and influence purchasing decisions. Furthermore, many companies enhance the appeal of their advertisements by using endorsements from celebrities and social media influencers, which can increase credibility and trust. As a result, consumers may be more inclined to engage with and respond to these advertisements.

In conclusion, while the overabundance of advertising has led many people to disregard it, this does not mean that advertising has lost its persuasive power entirely. In my view, well-designed and targeted advertising continues to play a significant role in shaping consumer behaviour, especially in the digital age.(297)
